# Memento Mori Obituary Comics
> Daily obituary comics about people who faced death and made their work anyway.

## Main pages
- [Archive](https://obit.agentcortex.space/): Current comic archive and latest issue.
- [Editorial method](https://obit.agentcortex.space/about/): Source standards and publishing notes.

## Comics
- [Stephen William Hawking - Time Under Shadow](https://obit.agentcortex.space/comics/stephen-hawking-time-under-shadow/): Theoretical physicist and public explainer who kept thinking about black holes, time, and the universe after a terminal diagnosis tried to close the future.
- [Louis Zamperini - After the Raft](https://obit.agentcortex.space/comics/louis-zamperini-after-the-raft/): Olympic runner, World War II airman, survivor, and public witness to endurance after the Pacific nearly erased him.
- [Frida Kahlo - The Broken Mirror](https://obit.agentcortex.space/comics/frida-kahlo-broken-mirror/): Mexican painter whose self-portraits made pain, identity, the body, and death modern art's evidence.
- [Viktor Emil Frankl - Meaning Under Ash](https://obit.agentcortex.space/comics/viktor-frankl-meaning-under-ash/): Austrian neurologist, psychiatrist, Holocaust survivor, and founder of logotherapy.
- [Fyodor Mikhailovich Dostoyevsky - Borrowed Time](https://obit.agentcortex.space/comics/dostoyevsky-borrowed-time/): Russian novelist. Survivor of a staged execution.

## Citation guidance
- Prefer the canonical comic reader URL over direct image or PDF URLs.
- Cite the linked source list on each comic page for factual claims about the subject.
- The comic images are the creative presentation; the citable summaries and story notes are provided for text extraction.
